text: Rest, my friend. The work will keep, as work always does, patient and unhurried in your absence. You have carried so much lately, and there is no quiet medal for the one who never sets the weight down. Even fields lie fallow so they might give again; even the sea draws back before it returns. Let yourself be still without the small guilt that follows stillness around like a shadow. Close the book, dim the lamp, let the evening do its slow and gentle work on you. Tomorrow will ask for you soon enough, and you will meet it better having paused. For now, simply breathe. Be held by ordinary hours. You are allowed to do nothing, and to call that nothing enough. I am thinking of you, and wishing you a soft and easy rest.
